REMARK: 304/EPS - DDC'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER SHORTENING THE NOTICE PERIOD AND RESPONSE TIME ON DEFENDANTS' DEMAND FOR AN INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION PURSUANT TO CCP 2032.220. THE COURT DENIES THE EX PARTE APPLICATION, WHICH SEEKS TO SHORTEN THE NOTICE AND RESPONSE TIME ON DEFENDANTS' DEMAND FOR AN INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OR TO SHORTEN TIME ON THE MOTION. THE IME WAS NOTICED FOR APRIL 24TH - WELL AFTER THE APRIL 7TH FACT DISCOVERY CUT OFF- AND NO EXPLANATION HAS BEEN OFFERED AS TO WHY THE IME COULD NOT HAVE BEEN TIMELY NOTICED. DEFENDANT WAITED OVER A YEAR AND A HALF AFTER THE CASE WAS FILED IN AUGUST 2023 TO RAISE THE ISSUE. DEFENDANT WAS ALSO PUT ON NOTICE ON FEBRUARY 27, 2025, THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD NOT INFORMALLY SUBMIT TO PROVIDING A BLOOD SAMPLE FOR DNA TESTING. UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE FOR THE DELAY, WHICH WAS WITHIN DEFENDANT'S CONTROL. EX PARTE RELIEF IS NOT WARRANTED UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs Punitive Damages Claim
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Akebono Brake Corporation's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR. Opposition filed. Reply filed.

Plaintiffs did not stipulate to Defendant providing less than the full 81-day statutory notice for its summary judgment. Plaintiffs argue that Defendant's motion is fatally defective because they did not receive sufficient notice of the motion as required under Code of Civil Procedure sections 437c(a)(2) and 1010.6(a)(4)(B). Plaintiffs contend that Defendant's motion is untimely served because Defendant failed to provide Plaintiffs with the requisite two additional court days for e-service. The Court agrees.

Code of Civil Procedure Section 437c(a)(2) provides that "notice of the motion and supporting papers shall be served on all other parties to the action at least 81 days before the time appointed for hearing." If the notice is served electronically, the required 81-day period of notice must be increased by two court days. (Code Civ. Proc., Sections 437c(a)(2), 1010.6(a)(4)(B).) The Court cannot shorten the notice period without the parties' prior consent. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 764; McMahon v. Super. Ct. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 117-118.) Nor can the Court cure the defect by continuing the hearing for the missing number of days. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267-1268.)

Here, Defendant's proof of service reflects that Defendant delivered its notice of motion by electronic service and filing on Thursday, January 10, 2025. However, the last day for such method of delivery for a hearing on April 1, 2025, was Wednesday, January 8, 2025.

Based on the foregoing, the notice of the motion was not timely served. Furthermore, the Court cannot cure the defect by continuing the motion to a later hearing date. Accordingly, Defendant's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar. The Court disagrees that Defendant's failure timely to serve its motion constitutes good cause to continue the trial date in this preference action.

The prevailing party is prepare and submit a proposed other that tracks the substantive portion of the tentative ruling verbatim. CRC 3.1312 compliance is required.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (304/EPS)
